perturbation.original_text: 1.1 ADVERTISING shall mean any paid advertisements, links, pointers, sponsorships, buttons, banners, navigation, or any other placements or promotions or similar services or rights on a Site, but excluding Advertising that is not paid for or which is part of an overall partnering or revenue sharing arrangement and any Product Listings.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 ADVERTISING shall mean any paid advertisements, links, pointers, sponsorships, buttons, banners, navigation, or any other placements or promotions or similar services or rights on a Site, but excluding Promotions that is not paid for or which is part of an overall partnering or revenue sharing arrangement and any Product Listings.
perturbation.explanation: The change involves swapping the word 'Advertising' with 'Promotions' within the definition of Advertising. This creates an internal contradiction because the definition is supposed to define 'Advertising', but it excludes 'Promotions' that fits its own definition. This directly contradicts Section 7, where 'Advertising' is used extensively, creating uncertainty if that section also applies to 'Promotions'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1.1 During the Term, VerticalNet shall have the non-exclusive right (except as to Neoforma) to arrange for the sale of Advertising on Neoforma Plan (and any other parts of the Neoforma Sites within which Neoforma elects to include Advertising) to third parties. During the Term, the parties shall meet from time to time to discuss the Advertising inventory available for sale. Any Advertising inventory that Neoforma appoints VerticalNet to arrange to sell shall not also be appointed to any third party to arrange for sale to third parties.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Advertising' clearly excludes 'Advertising' that isn't paid for. The changed text replaces 'Advertising' with 'Promotions' in this exclusion, implying that 'Promotions' that aren't paid for are excluded from the definition of 'Advertising.' However, Section 7.1.1 grants VerticalNet the right to sell 'Advertising.' If 'Promotions' falls under the definition of 'Advertising,' excluding unpaid promotions while also giving VerticalNet the right to sell 'Advertising' (which could now include some promotions) creates a contradiction about whether VerticalNet has the right to sell unpaid 'Promotions'. This uncertainty directly impacts the enforceability of section 7.1.1, as it's unclear what 'Advertising' includes.

perturbation.original_text: 1.3 CAREER CENTER GROSS MARGIN shall have the meaning ascribed thereto in Section 10.5.1 [CO-BRANDED CAREER CENTER].
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 CAREER CENTER GROSS MARGIN shall have the meaning ascribed thereto in Section 10.5.1 [CO-BRANDED TRAINING CENTER].
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the cross-reference, pointing 'CAREER CENTER GROSS MARGIN' to Section 10.5.1, which is about 'CO-BRANDED TRAINING CENTER', rather than 'CO-BRANDED CAREER CENTER'. This creates a direct conflict and introduces uncertainty of what constitutes Career Center Gross Margin. The contradiction directly creates an intext contradiction in Section 10.5.1 because it misdirects the definition. The confusion can also lead to dispute in how to calculate the figures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.5.1 CO-BRANDED CAREER CENTER. VerticalNet will pay Neoforma [*] of the Career Center Gross Margin. "Career Center Gross Margin" shall mean the listing fees related to the Neoforma Career Content and e-commerce revenue derived during the Term from users of the Co-Branded Career Center (less Deductibles).
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'CAREER CENTER GROSS MARGIN' by incorrectly cross-referencing it to Section 10.5.1 [CO-BRANDED TRAINING CENTER] instead of Section 10.5.1 [CO-BRANDED CAREER CENTER]. The contradicted text, Section 10.5.1, defines 'Career Center Gross Margin' in relation to the 'Co-Branded Career Center'. This misdirection directly contradicts the definition in Section 10.5.1, causing a conflict and uncertainty regarding the correct calculation of 'Career Center Gross Margin', thereby aligning with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.37 NEW MEDICAL PRODUCTS shall mean new, unused Medical Products.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.37 NEW MEDICAL PRODUCTS shall mean used, reconditioned Medical Products.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of New Medical Products is altered to mean 'used, reconditioned Medical Products'. This creates a contradiction as the term 'New' implies unused, conflicting with 'used, reconditioned'. This will create a conflict with SECTION 2 where New Medical Products is referenced, by altering its definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Within 30 days after the Effective Date, VerticalNet shall provide a copy of all VerticalNet Medical Product Listings for New Medical Products existing as of the Effective Date to Neoforma for use on Neoforma Plan, Neoforma Shop and Neoforma Auction on an exclusive basis (even as to VerticalNet), to the extent VerticalNet has the right to do so. VerticalNet shall use commercially reasonable efforts to acquire consent from its customers to provide all VerticalNet Medical Product Listings for New Medical Products existing as of the Effective Date to Neoforma.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'NEW MEDICAL PRODUCTS' to mean 'used, reconditioned Medical Products'. This directly contradicts the ordinary understanding of 'new' as unused. SECTION 2.2 refers to 'New Medical Products existing as of the Effective Date', implying unused products. The modified definition now states those 'New Medical Products' are used and reconditioned, creating a direct in-text contradiction. This means VerticalNet would provide a copy of all VerticalNet Medical Product Listings for *used* Medical Products when the clear implication of section 2.2 is for *new* medical products.
